title: GUM managed-scope manifest schema (v1)
description: JSON Schema 2020-12 validation for apps/gum/internal/embedded/data/auth-managed-scopes.v1.json. Enforces the §7 promotion procedure's preconditions at parse time so that an invalid manifest fails the build before cmd/gen-catalog evaluates gum_oauth variants. Updates to this schema MUST land in the same PR as the corresponding spec.md §7 change.

_preamble.description: Informative pointer to the governing spec.md procedure; not consumed at runtime.

schema_version.description: Bumped on incompatible field-shape changes to the manifest format. The v0.1.0 catalog generator pins to 1; future versions require a documented migration path.

embedded_client_secret.description: The managed gum_oauth strategy manifest never carries an OAuth client secret. Release builds may still include Google Desktop client material injected by the build system for the default managed BYO OAuth fallback; that material is not committed to source.

scopes.description: List of OAuth scopes the GUM-managed OAuth client may request. v0.1.0 release tagging requires at least one entry with status=active (see GUM_OAUTH_MANAGED_CLIENT_NOT_READY in spec.md §7).

scope.description: Full OAuth scope URI. v0.1.0 supports only googleapis.com scopes; sovereign-cloud scope hosts (e.g. mil/gov) are deferred to v0.4.0 universe-domain support.

service.description: Short service identifier matching the catalog's service field (e.g. gmail, drive, calendar). Used for service-scoped diagnostics.

category.description: Google's OAuth scope sensitivity classification. 'restricted' scopes (gmail.*, drive.*) have stricter consent and verification requirements than 'sensitive' scopes.

status.description: active = managed client may request this scope today. planned = listed for documentation but NOT eligible for gum_oauth requests.

testing_allowed.description: Explicit per-scope opt-in for the Google 'testing' publishing window. When managed_project.publishing_status is 'testing', a scope with testing_allowed=true is eligible for gum_oauth before full verification completes (<=100-user cap, unverified-app warning). Inert under any other publishing_status, so production stays strict by default. Defaults to false when omitted.

allOf.$comment: If status is active, all four readiness states must be at their positive terminal value. Enforces the spec.md §7 promotion procedure at the schema layer.
